channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Department of Homeland Security will suspend TSA PreCheck and Global Entry as shutdown continues
item.description: The temporary suspension of the two popular programs that allow some travelers to move more quickly through airport security will start 6 a.m. Sunday. It is one of several emergency measures DHS said it is taking to redirect staffing more than a week after Congress failed to send more money to the agency.

item.title: A powerful storm will hit some cities with a foot of snow. See the forecast for 12.
item.description: Blizzard conditions will lash parts of the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast, where nearly 100 million people can expect moderate-to-extreme impacts.

item.title: These activities could help postpone Alzheimer’s by 5 years, study suggests
item.description: The survey, one of the largest of its kind, promotes the idea that the texture of daily life can leave a measurable imprint on the aging brain.

item.title: Powerful coastal storm to bring snow, wind to D.C. area, with the worst arriving Sunday night
item.description: Snow could continue into Monday and cause delays and closings. Precipitation will begin as mix of rain and snow early Sunday morning.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: At ‘wit’s end’ over adult daughters’ messy personal lives
item.description: Carolyn Hax: At ‘wit’s end’ over adult daughters’ messy personal lives

item.title: Trump raises his global tariff to 15%, beginning new chapter of economic uncertainty after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: Even as the court determined Friday that the president had exceeded his authority by slapping tariffs on goods from just about every country in the world, President Donald Trump has made clear that he is determined to do so again, though this time within the bounds of the law.

item.title: Trump aides struggle with how to spend $500 billion more on military
item.description: The White House budget chief was among those who internally objected to the defense secretary’s plan to increase military spending by roughly 50 percent, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: Justice Department fires U.S. attorney in Eastern District of Virginia shortly after his appointment by federal judges
item.description: Federal judges appointed longtime litigator James W. Hundley to serve as interim U.S. attorney earlier Friday. Hundley would have succeeded Lindsey Halligan, a Trump administration lawyer whose appointment was found to be improper.

item.title: Column: Restaurants, stop explaining everything to us
item.description: When did dinner out start to feel like a TED Talk?

item.title: He made a fake ICE deportation tip line. Then a kindergarten teacher called.
item.description: What began as a comedy routine has become one of the most viral pieces of social satire during President Donald Trump’s mass-deportation campaign.

item.title: Trump’s tariffs fall to a principled Supreme Court
item.description: The decision is a triumph for the separation of powers and individual liberty, the Editorial Board writes.

item.title: D.C. area could see snow Sunday into Monday. How much could fall?
item.description: Because of the possibility of heavy snow, the National Weather Service has issued a winter storm watch from Sunday afternoon into Monday morning for D.C.'s far northern and northwest suburbs, including western Loudoun, western Montgomery, western Howard, Frederick and Carroll counties.

item.title: That’s the end of British reverence for the monarchy, I’m sorry to say
item.description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s arrest marks an irrevocable downturn in the royal family’s stature, Tim Stanley writes.

item.title: Trump denounces Supreme Court justices who ruled against him as a ‘disgrace’ and announces wide-ranging new tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump said that in response to the Supreme Court striking down his sweeping tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, he will impose new global tariffs under different statutes.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: Is that what he calls a ‘healthy’ relationship with his adult kids?
item.description: Carolyn Hax chat: Is that what he calls a ‘healthy’ relationship with his adult kids?

item.title: What the Supreme Court ruling against Trump’s tariffs means for you
item.description: The high court ruled Friday that most of President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs put in place last year are invalid — but that doesn’t mean shoppers will suddenly see prices drop.

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down most of Trump's tariffs in rare rebuke of president
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down most of President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariffs, a ruling that deals a major blow to his signature economic policy and represents a stinging political setback.

item.title: 10 questions, and don't forget the bonus wager! Finish off your week with On the Record.
item.description: Think you’ve got what it takes to earn a perfect score? Take today’s longer news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: See where snow could be measured in inches — or feet — in the East this weekend
item.description: Accumulating snow, strong winds and travel disruptions are possible from Sunday into Monday.

item.title: U.S. economic growth slowed sharply at the end of 2025, dragging down the year
item.description: New GDP figures reflect subdued growth from new trade policies and a weeks-long government shutdown.

item.title: Iran preparations. Trump disapproval. Election fraud. WHO replacement. Storm forecast. Alysa Liu. ‘Brain rot.’ Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Alysa Liu wins gold for U.S. in figure skating, completing incredible comeback
item.description: Liu’s gold is the first individual women’s medal for the United States since 2006 in Turin.

item.title: U.S. women’s hockey wins gold medal in overtime stunner over Canada
item.description: Trailing for most of the game, the U.S. scored late in the third period and again in overtime to secure the victory.

item.title: Incredibly, men are still wearing ‘the George Washington’
item.description: Incredibly, men are still wearing ‘the George Washington’

item.title: Trump appears ready to attack Iran as U.S. strike force takes shape
item.description: The arsenal, under assembly for weeks, is awaiting the arrival of the aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford and its accompanying warships, officials familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Ilia Malinin says person who came to Olympics a few weeks ago is ‘dust’
item.description: Gone is the shy 21-year-old who talked effusively about figure skating, replaced by a young man whose words come in measured and concrete thoughts about a larger purpose.

item.title: After WHO exit, Trump officials propose replacement that will cost U.S. about three times as much
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services proposes spending $2 billion a year to recreate systems the U.S. accessed through the World Health Organization at a fraction of the cost, according to officials briefed on the matter.

item.title: Commission packed with Trump allies approves White House ballroom plans as judge considers whether to halt project
item.description: The Commission of Fine Arts voted unanimously to approve the nearly 90,000-square-foot building’s design, which would be the most significant change to the White House complex in decades.

item.title: Leaving ‘60 Minutes’ in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: U.S. trade deficits remain high in 2025, despite Trump’s tariff policy
item.description: Fresh data released by the Commerce Department showed the full-year total deficit combining goods and services was $901.5 billion, down a smidgen from 2024 but still one of the highest levels in decades.

item.title: Board of Peace. Former prince Andrew. Refugee vetting. Avalanche. College pacts. Alzheimer’s ‘clock.’ Olympics. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Former prince Andrew arrested on suspicion of misconduct in office, news reports say
item.description: U.K. police announced an investigation but did not name Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, who was stripped of his titles last year over his connections to Jeffrey Epstein. The arrest was reported by the BBC and Associated Press.

item.title: Homeland Security memo outlines plan to arrest refugees lacking a green card, detain them for rescreening
item.description: The guidance is a policy shift that upends decades of protections and puts tens of thousands of people who entered the country during the Biden administration at risk. The new policy rescinds a 2010 memo that said failing to apply for status as a lawful permanent resident within a year of living in the United States is not a basis for detaining refugees who entered the country legally.

item.title: D.C. mayor declares Potomac sewage spill an emergency, seeks federal aid for cleanup
item.description: Muriel E. Bowser's administration hopes the move will free up federal funds to reimburse local authorities for repair and recovery costs.

item.title: The $1,000-a-month vehicle payment is becoming the norm. Why are cars getting so expensive?
item.description: Prices are at record highs. More loans are going bad. It’s not an easy time to afford a new car in America.

item.title: 8 skiers killed in deadliest avalanche in modern California history; search for 9th continues
item.description: It surpasses the toll of seven killed in a March 1982 avalanche at the Alpine Meadows — now Palisades Tahoe — ski resort, in the same general region.

item.title: White House taps Jay Bhattacharya, a critic of the CDC, to lead the agency for now
item.description: The co-author of the Great Barrington Declaration, which rebuked the CDC’s response to the coronavirus pandemic, will oversee the nation’s leading public health agency.

item.title: Trump officials limit FEMA travel to disaster areas amid funding lapse, emails show
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security has halted almost all travel amid the ongoing standoff over its funding, restricting the ability of some Federal Emergency Management Agency staff to move in and out of disaster-affected areas, according to emails and documents obtained by The Washington Post.

item.title: Partner is cringingly kind to strangers. Carolyn Hax readers give advice.
item.description: Letter writer worries about being “too critical” of partner’s eagerness to help strangers, who sometimes get visibly annoyed.

item.title: It took one year for Black MAGA to crater
item.description: The African Americans who voted for Trump are no longer feeling the “love.” Here’s why.

item.title: Signs of alien life in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Wednesday points.

item.title: FDA reverses course and will review Moderna’s mRNA-based flu shot
item.description: Moderna announced that the FDA agreed to review its application for a mRNA-based flu vaccine after the company agreed to further studies. Federal officials did not immediately comment.

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin wins gold in slalom, emphatically ends her Olympic drought
item.description: The most decorated Alpine skier in World Cup history delivered in her final event of the Milan Cortina Games with a dominant performance.

item.title: ICE restrictions. Pete Hegseth. Colbert vs. CBS. Missing skiers. Expensive cars. Atlantic essay. Superhuman balance. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: The Epstein files have brought consequences for some powerful people. Here's a list.
item.description: A number of prominent figures have stepped down or are facing investigations after their communications with Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell, his former longtime companion, were released last month.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Partner feels like an afterthought on his busy life calendar
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Partner feels like an afterthought on his busy life calendar

item.title: A newspaper in this city is the topic of today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Tuesday points.

item.title: Hegseth forces ouster of senior Army spokesman in latest internal clash
item.description: It marks Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s latest move to force out an officer who worked for retired Gen. Mark A. Milley, a target of President Donald Trump.

item.title: Jesse Jackson. Iran talks. Epstein files. Slavery exhibit. Obama and aliens. Keto diet. Sleep and creativity. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.
